steps.title: Fit the doubling pattern
steps.content: The bacteria count starts at $44{,}000$, so the initial value is $44{,}000$. Doubling every day means each daily multiplier is $2$.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: An exponential model has the form $y=a(b)^t$, where $a$ is the starting value and $b$ is the multiplier per time period. Here $a=44{,}000$ and $b=2$, so $$y=44{,}000(2)^t$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D has the correct starting value and the doubling factor. Choices A and B use 44,000 as the exponential base, and choice C models halving instead of doubling. Choice D is correct.
